repair_cases.c1_complaint: Customer reports intermittent engine misfire at idle and acceleration. Check engine light on. Worse on cold start.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0301 confirmed active. Cylinder 1 spark plug worn, gap 0.9mm (BMW limit 0.6mm). Ignition coil resistance 12.4 ohms (within spec). No wiring damage found.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced cylinder 1 spark plug (Part #12120036244). Cleared DTCs. Road tested 5 miles — no misfire. MIL extinguished. Misfire counter at zero.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Always check coil before condemning plug. Verify gap with feeler gauge.
common_mistakes: Skipping coil resistance check
Not recording electrode gap measurement
Forgetting part number on C3

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Car shudders when accelerating from stop. Engine management light intermittent.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0301 stored. Misfire count 847 in last drive cycle. Ignition coil cylinder 1 faulty — primary resistance 0.4 ohms (limit 0.5-0.8 ohms). Spark plug within spec.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced ignition coil cylinder 1 (Part #12138657273). Cleared DTCs. Road tested 8 miles — no misfire. Misfire counters zero across all cylinders.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0301 on B48 — test coil primary resistance first before replacing plug.
common_mistakes: Replacing plug without testing coil
Not verifying misfire counter in live data

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Engine takes longer than usual to warm up. Temperature gauge slow to rise. Fuel economy decreased.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0128 active. Thermostat function test: coolant reached only 78C after 15 min (spec min 87C). Thermostat confirmed stuck open.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced thermostat assembly (Part #A2702000115). Coolant bled per MB WIS. Engine reaches 90C within 8 minutes. Cleared DTCs. No codes after 10-mile road test.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Always record actual temp reached and time taken — MB warranty requires this for P0128.
common_mistakes: Not recording actual temperature readings
Skipping coolant bleed procedure

repair_cases.c1_complaint: MIL on. Customer unaware of drivability issues.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0420. Post-cat O2 showing switching activity matching pre-cat — catalyst efficiency loss confirmed. No upstream exhaust leaks found.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced catalytic converter bank 1 (Part #25051-0P120). Toyota drive cycle completed: 2 cold starts + 20 miles. P0420 not present. Post-cat sensor showing correct flat waveform.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Toyota P0420 — complete full drive cycle before closing RO.
common_mistakes: Insufficient drive cycle
Not comparing O2 waveforms before condemning cat

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Rough idle and hesitation on acceleration. Check engine light on.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0171 — system lean bank 1. LTFT bank 1 at +22% (limit +10%). Smoke test: vacuum leak at intake manifold gasket near cylinder 3. MAF normal. O2 functioning correctly.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced intake manifold gasket set (Part #BR3Z-9439-A). LTFT bank 1 returned to +3% after idle. Road tested — no hesitation. Post-repair smoke test confirmed no leaks.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0171 on EcoBoost — smoke test before touching MAF. Record LTFT before and after.
common_mistakes: Replacing MAF before smoke test
Not recording LTFT values

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Significant power loss especially on motorway.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0299 — turbo underboost. Boost at WOT: 0.6 bar (spec 1.3 bar). Pressure test: split charge air cooler inlet hose confirmed, 40% pressure loss in 10 seconds.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced charge air cooler inlet hose (Part #8K0145832N). Boost test: 1.28 bar at WOT. Cleared DTCs. Road test including motorway — full boost confirmed.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0299 on VAG — pressure test boost system before condemning turbo.
common_mistakes: Condemning turbo without boost leak test
Missing actual boost pressure reading

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Random misfire at idle. Shaking at traffic lights. Worse in wet weather.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0300. Ignition lead 3 found with cracked insulation. HV leak confirmed with UV light. Spark plugs within gap specification.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced full ignition lead set (Part #32722-5BA-A00). Ran engine in wet conditions — no misfire. Road tested. P0300 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Random misfire in wet weather — always check HT leads with UV light.
common_mistakes: Not using UV light for HT lead inspection

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Check engine light on. No drivability issues.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0455 — large EVAP leak. Fuel cap pressure test failed at 0.5 PSI (spec 1.0 PSI min). Fuel cap seal cracked and worn.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced fuel cap (Part #A2224700805). EVAP leak test: held 1.2 PSI for 60 seconds. Road tested. P0455 not present after monitor completion.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Always check fuel cap first on P0455 — simple fix 40% of the time.
common_mistakes: Skipping fuel cap test
Not performing post-repair leak test

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 3-4 seconds. No other drivability concerns.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0011 — intake cam timing over-advanced bank 1. Oil pressure to VANOS: 2.1 bar (spec 2.5 bar min). Oil level low by 0.8L. Oil dark and degraded — overdue by 12,000 miles. VANOS solenoid resistance within spec.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Full synthetic oil and filter service (8.5L Shell Helix Ultra 5W-30 Part #11427953125). Cleared DTCs. Cold start — rattle absent. Advised customer on service interval. P0011 did not return after 15-minute road test.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Check oil level and condition first on all VANOS codes.
common_mistakes: Condemning VANOS solenoid without checking oil level

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Hybrid warning light on. Car occasionally drops to EV-only mode unexpectedly.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0A0F — drive motor performance. HV battery SOC normal at 68%. Cell voltage imbalance: cell group 4 at 3.6V vs others at 3.85V. Hybrid control module data logged.
repair_cases.c3_correction: HV battery module replacement per Toyota TB-0089-22 (Part #G9280-0R011). Post-replacement cell balance: all groups within 0.05V. Full hybrid cycle test completed. No fault codes.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Always reference specific Toyota TB number on hybrid battery claims.
common_mistakes: Missing TB reference number
Not logging pre-repair cell voltage data

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Customer states engine stumbles and shakes on first start of the day. Misfire felt throughout entire RPM range. CEL flashing on cold start, goes steady after warm-up.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs P0300 and P0316 confirmed active. P0316 indicates misfire on first 1000 engine revolutions — cold start misfire pattern. Misfire distributed across cylinders 2, 4, 7 in freeze frame. Fuel injectors 2, 4, 7 dynamic flow test: 20-22% below spec (spec ±5%). Carbon buildup visible on injector tips via borescope.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Cleaned fuel injectors using Ford-approved MOTORVAC system per TSB 21-2214. Post-clean flow balance within 3%. Cleared DTCs. Cold start cycle x3 — no misfire. P0316 and P0300 monitors cleared after 22-mile road test including highway run.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0316 is a cold start misfire flag — combine with P0300 to identify injector carbon deposits on direct injection engines. Always perform injector flow test before ordering parts.
common_mistakes: Replacing coils without testing injector flow
Not referencing P0316 alongside P0300 in C2
Missing borescope step — injector carbon not visible externally

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Check engine light on. Customer reports occasional engine ping under load on the highway. No smoke or unusual noise at idle.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0325 — knock sensor 1 circuit malfunction. Knock sensor output voltage at idle: 0.3V (spec 0.5-4.5V dynamic range). Wiring harness inspected — connector pins 1 and 2 corroded at sensor connector. No other DTCs. ECM knock retard data showing 4.2 degrees retard at WOT.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Cleaned connector pins with electrical contact cleaner, applied dielectric grease. Rechecked sensor voltage — 0.6V rising to 2.8V under rev. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 15 miles including WOT pull — knock retard 0.0 degrees. P0325 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0325 on Toyota — inspect connector first before condemning sensor. Corrosion at the 2-pin connector is the most common root cause on M20A engines.
common_mistakes: Replacing knock sensor without checking connector condition
Not measuring sensor output voltage dynamically
Forgetting to record degree of knock retard pre/post repair

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Vehicle cranks but will not start. Intermittently starts after multiple attempts. No warning lights prior to no-start condition.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0340 — camshaft position sensor circuit bank 1. Sensor signal voltage at cranking: 0.1V (spec 5.0V reference, 0V-5V switching). Wiring continuity test: open circuit at pin 3 of sensor connector — chafed harness against cam cover edge. Sensor resistance 860 ohms (within spec).
repair_cases.c3_correction: Repaired wiring harness at chafe point with heat-shrink solder splice (Part #WHT004900). Applied split loom protection over repaired section. Cleared DTCs. Vehicle starts immediately on first crank. Road tested 12 miles — no stall, no return of P0340.

parts_used.description: Wiring repair kit, heat shrink solder connectors

repair_cases.technician_notes: P0340 on VAG — harness chafe against cam cover is a known failure point on EA888 Gen4. Check before ordering sensor. Always document the chafe location in C2.
common_mistakes: Ordering camshaft sensor before testing circuit continuity
Not documenting exact location of harness damage
Forgetting to add protective loom over repair

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Customer states fuel economy noticeably worse over last 3 months. No drivability concerns. MIL illuminated.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0401 — EGR flow insufficient. EGR valve functional test: valve commanded 100% open, actual position sensor reads 38% (spec >90%). EGR cooler differential pressure: 0.08 bar (spec 0.18-0.25 bar). Carbon deposit buildup in EGR passage confirmed by borescope — 65% blockage estimated.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Removed and cleaned EGR cooler and valve assembly per BMW SI B11 06 21. EGR passage cleaned with approved solvent. Post-clean valve test: actual position 94% at 100% command. Cleared DTCs. Road test 18 miles — P0401 not present. Fuel economy advised will improve over 500-mile break-in.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0401 on B58 — always record EGR valve actual vs commanded position. Document borescope findings with photos for warranty claim.
common_mistakes: Replacing EGR valve without cleaning passage
Not photographing borescope findings
Missing pre and post valve position data

repair_cases.c1_complaint: MIL illuminated for 2 weeks. Customer reports no performance issues. Vehicle has no aftermarket exhaust modifications.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0420 — catalyst efficiency below threshold bank 1. Pre-cat O2: normal switching 0.1V-0.9V at 0.5Hz. Post-cat O2: switching at 0.45Hz — approaching pre-cat frequency, efficiency loss confirmed. Exhaust upstream leaks: none found. Pre-cat O2 heater current: 1.8A (within spec). Mileage 52,000 — within 8-year/80,000-mile federal emissions warranty.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced catalytic converter bank 1 (Part #A2774909600) per MB emissions warranty. Performed MB-approved drive cycle: 2 cold starts, 35 minutes mixed driving. Post-cat O2 waveform: flat line at 0.65V — correct. P0420 monitor passed. No DTCs present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Always verify mileage against federal 8/80 emissions warranty before authorizing repair. Record O2 waveform frequency pre and post replacement — required for MB warranty documentation.
common_mistakes: Not referencing federal emissions warranty coverage
Missing O2 waveform frequency documentation
Insufficient drive cycle before closing

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Check engine light on. No change in performance noted by customer. Vehicle passed state emissions test 14 months ago.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0430 — catalyst efficiency below threshold bank 2. Post-cat O2 bank 2 waveform switching at 0.38Hz (spec: <0.1Hz for healthy catalyst). Upstream exhaust manifold gasket inspection — small leak found at cylinder 4 exhaust port, 0.5mm gap confirmed with feeler gauge. Gasket leak contributing to false lean readings.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced exhaust manifold gasket bank 2 (Part #06K253039J). Rechecked post-cat O2 — waveform switching reduced to 0.22Hz. Catalyst efficiency marginal but within threshold with leak eliminated. Cleared DTCs. Drive cycle completed — P0430 monitor passed. No return of code after 400-mile extended road test.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0430 on VAG — always inspect upstream exhaust gaskets before condemning catalyst. A small exhaust leak can trigger P0430 without catalyst failure.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — exhaust gasket failure outside powertrain warranty period.
common_mistakes: Replacing catalyst without finding upstream leak
Not recording O2 waveform switching frequency
Not confirming fix with extended road test

repair_cases.c1_complaint: MIL on. Customer reports faint fuel smell occasionally when parked. No drivability issues.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0442 — small EVAP leak. Fuel cap test passed at 1.1 PSI. EVAP smoke machine test: smoke identified at purge solenoid outlet hose junction — cracked hose at clamp location. EVAP canister integrity confirmed. No other EVAP component leaks.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced EVAP purge solenoid outlet hose (Part #CM5Z-9C015-A). Smoke test repeated — no leaks found. EVAP system held 1.0 PSI for 90 seconds. Cleared DTCs. Drive cycle including idle and cruise — EVAP monitor passed. P0442 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0442 small EVAP leak — always use smoke machine. Purge hose at clamp location is a common failure on 1.5 EcoBoost. Document smoke test findings in C2.
common_mistakes: Not using smoke machine for small EVAP leaks
Replacing purge solenoid instead of hose
Not performing post-repair smoke confirmation

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Engine idle speed surges between 500 and 1100 RPM. Customer states AC use makes it worse. No other warning lights.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0507 — idle air control system high RPM. Throttle body bore inspection: significant carbon deposit coating on throttle plate and bore walls. At idle, throttle position 2.1% (spec: 0.8-1.2%). IAC learned value at maximum correction limit. No vacuum leaks found on smoke test.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Throttle body cleaning performed — throttle plate and bore cleaned with Honda-approved throttle body cleaner. IAC idle learn procedure performed via HDS scan tool. Post-procedure idle: 720 RPM steady (spec 700-750 RPM). Cleared DTCs. Road tested with AC on — idle stable at 780 RPM under AC load. P0507 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0507 on Honda 1.5L — carbon throttle body is the primary cause. Must perform idle learn procedure via HDS after cleaning or idle will remain unstable. Do not use generic throttle body sprays — use Honda-approved cleaner only.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — carbon buildup from extended oil change intervals. Oil change overdue by 4,200 miles.
common_mistakes: Skipping idle learn procedure after cleaning
Using non-approved cleaner that can damage throttle bore coating
Not documenting pre-clean throttle position vs spec

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Intermittent warning: 'Increased battery discharge' on iDrive. Vehicle slow to start occasionally. Radio resets. Stop/start system disabled.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0562 — system voltage low. Battery voltage at idle: 12.1V (spec 13.8-14.4V). Alternator output tested: 12.4V (spec 14.0-14.8V). Load test at 50A: voltage drops to 11.2V — alternator below output spec. Battery load test: 550 CCA measured vs 600 CCA rated — marginal. Serpentine belt tension within spec.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced alternator (Part #12317823530) and battery (Part #61219393955, 90Ah AGM) per BMW charging system replacement protocol. Performed BMW battery registration via ISTA. Post-replacement charge voltage: 14.3V at idle, 14.6V at 2000 RPM. Load test at 50A: 13.9V. Road tested 20 miles — no voltage warnings. Stop/start functional.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0562 on BMW — always test alternator output under load, not just at idle. Battery registration via ISTA is mandatory after battery replacement or stop/start will not function correctly.
common_mistakes: Replacing battery only without testing alternator
Forgetting battery registration step via ISTA
Not recording load test voltage — required for BMW warranty claim

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Vehicle would not start after sitting overnight at airport. Jump-start required. All settings reset. Adaptive cruise and suspension memory lost.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0603 — ECM internal control module keep alive memory error. Battery voltage before jump: 9.8V (spec min 12.4V). Battery CCA test: 420 CCA vs 800 CCA rated — failed. Deep discharge event confirmed by battery management system log. ECM keep alive memory corrupted from undervoltage.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced battery (Part #A0009829208, 92Ah AGM). Registered new battery via MB XENTRY. ECM variant coding verified — all parameters intact per coding backup. Road test 15 miles. All settings relearned. P0603 not present after full power cycle. No further DTCs.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0603 on MB M256 — deep discharge from battery failure. Always back up ECM variant coding before battery work on M256 platform. Battery registration via XENTRY is non-negotiable.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — battery failure from extended parking with high vampire drain accessories.
common_mistakes: Not backing up ECM coding before battery replacement
Skipping battery registration in XENTRY
Not checking battery CCA vs rated spec

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Transmission slipping between 2nd and 3rd gear under moderate acceleration. No warning lights initially — now transmission warning illuminated.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0700 — transmission control system MIL request. P0730 — gear ratio incorrect. ATF inspection: fluid dark brown, burned odor, particulate matter on dipstick (spec: light red/pink, no particles). ATF level: correct. Pressure test 2-3 clutch pack: 98 PSI (spec 135-155 PSI). Clutch C2 hydraulic circuit pressure low — suspected worn clutch packs.
repair_cases.c3_correction: ATF drain and refill with Toyota WS fluid (3x flush, Part #00289-ATFWS) performed first. Pressure retest — 2-3 clutch pack 102 PSI — marginally improved but still below spec. Transmission replacement authorized per Toyota warranty claim. Installed remanufactured transmission assembly (Part #35000-0E370). ATF filled to specification. Road test — gear changes smooth, no slip. P0700 and P0730 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0700 + P0730 on 2GR-FKS Highlander — always check ATF condition and pressure test clutch packs before condemning transmission. Document fluid condition with photos for warranty authorization.
common_mistakes: Not performing ATF flush before authorizing transmission replacement
Missing clutch pack pressure test data
Forgetting to photograph ATF condition for warranty

repair_cases.c1_complaint: CEL on. Slight rough idle noticeable when AC is on. Customer states fuel economy has dropped approximately 2 MPG.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P1101 — MAF out of self test range. MAF actual vs expected: 6.2 g/s at idle (spec 7.0-8.5 g/s). Air filter inspection: severely restricted — 2x normal differential pressure across filter element. STFT at +14% (spec ±10%). Intake tract inspected — no leaks. MAF connector and wiring intact.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced air filter element (Part #CJ5Z-9601-A). Cleared DTCs. MAF at idle: 7.8 g/s — within spec. STFT: +2%. Road tested 10 miles. P1101 not present. Customer advised on air filter change interval (every 15,000 miles per Ford maintenance schedule).
repair_cases.technician_notes: P1101 on Ford — always check air filter before condemning MAF. A blocked filter creates low MAF signal identical to sensor failure. Record MAF g/s values before and after repair.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — maintenance neglect. Air filter 28,000 miles overdue.
common_mistakes: Ordering MAF sensor before inspecting air filter
Not recording MAF g/s values pre and post repair
Skipping STFT verification after filter replacement

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Multiple warning lights on: ESC, ABS, steering. Adaptive cruise control disabled. Occasional hesitation on acceleration.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC U0100 — lost communication with ECM/PCM. Network scan: CAN bus voltage on high speed CAN (HS-CAN): 1.8V dominant, 3.9V recessive (spec: 1.5V/3.5V). CAN termination resistance measured: 60 ohms (spec 60 ohms — correct). Fault originated at ECM ground point G201 — measured 1.4 ohms resistance (spec <0.1 ohms). Corroded ground strap at G201 location on engine block.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Cleaned ECM ground point G201 with wire brush, applied NO-OX-ID terminal protectant. Re-torqued ground strap to 10 Nm. G201 resistance: 0.02 ohms. Cleared all DTCs via ODIS. All modules communicating — no U-codes present. Road tested 15 miles. No recurrence.
repair_cases.technician_notes: U0100 on VAG — check ECM ground points before any module replacement. G201 on the 3.0 TFSI is a known corrosion point. Always measure ground resistance with a milliohm meter, not a standard multimeter.
common_mistakes: Replacing ECM without checking ground resistance
Using standard multimeter for ground resistance (not sensitive enough)
Not clearing all modules after ground repair

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Noticeable shake at idle. Engine management light flashing during first 10 minutes of driving. Customer states it started after recent fuel fill-up.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs P0300 random and P0303 cylinder 3 misfire. Misfire rate cylinder 3: 4.2% per 100 revolutions (BMW flag threshold >1%). Fuel sample taken — ethanol content 28% (standard pump fuel spec E10, max 10%). Water contamination: 0.4% by volume. Injector and coil cylinder 3 tested within spec.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Drained contaminated fuel — 48L removed. Tank flushed with 5L clean unleaded per BMW procedure. Refilled with 50L premium unleaded, ethanol confirmed <5%. Cleared DTCs. Engine misfires absent after refill. Road tested 20 miles — no misfire return. Customer advised to use branded fuel stations.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Misfire after fuel fill — always test ethanol content and water contamination before replacing ignition components. B38 is sensitive to ethanol above E10.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — fuel contamination, not a vehicle defect.
common_mistakes: Replacing coil and plug before testing fuel quality
Not recording ethanol percentage measurement in C2
Forgetting to document fuel drain volume

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Customer states occasional pinging sound under load. MIL on. No loss of power noticed.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0325 — knock sensor 1 malfunction. Knock sensor resistance: 542k ohms (spec 400-650k ohms — within range). Output signal under 2000 RPM rev: 0.08V peak (spec: 0.5V-3.0V). Sensor mounting torque checked: 8 Nm (spec 17-23 Nm — significantly undertorqued). Sensor body loose in block — insufficient clamping contact causing low signal amplitude.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Removed knock sensor, inspected seating surface — no damage. Reinstalled and torqued to 20 Nm per Honda spec. Rechecked output signal: 1.2V-2.4V dynamic range under engine load. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 12 miles including WOT acceleration — knock retard 0 degrees. P0325 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0325 — always verify knock sensor torque spec before condemning. Loose sensor is indistinguishable from failed sensor in output voltage.
common_mistakes: Replacing sensor without verifying installation torque
Not measuring output voltage amplitude under engine load
Missing torque spec documentation in C2

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Transmission warning lamp on. Customer reports delayed engagement when selecting Drive from Park. Hybrid boost feels reduced.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0700 and P175F — transmission clutch actuator malfunction. Porsche PASM diagnostic: clutch K2 actuator response time 380ms (spec <120ms). Transmission fluid temperature normal at 82C. ATF fluid condition good — light amber, no particulates. Actuator solenoid resistance: 4.2 ohms (spec 3.5-5.0 ohms). Internal actuator hydraulic valve stuck — contamination suspected.
repair_cases.c3_correction: ATF flush x2 with Porsche-approved Pentosin ATF 1 LT (Part #000043204J3). Actuator valve freed after fluid flush. Retested actuator K2 response time: 95ms — within spec. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 18 miles including Porsche hybrid charge/discharge cycle. No fault return. Customer advised 30,000-mile ATF service interval.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P175F on Porsche hybrid — ATF contamination causing slow actuator response. Always perform fluid flush before condemning actuator assembly. Document actuator response time pre and post repair.
common_mistakes: Ordering clutch actuator assembly before ATF flush
Not measuring actuator response time in milliseconds
Missing pre-flush ATF condition documentation

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Engine misfires badly on cold start — shaking violently for 5-10 seconds. Calms down once warm. CEL flashing on startup.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs P0316, P0301, P0302. Cold start misfire concentrated cylinders 1 and 2. Coil-on-plug resistance both cylinders within spec. Borescope inspection cylinders 1 and 2: severe carbon buildup on intake valves — estimated 3mm deposit thickness. Direct injection engine, no port wash. Fuel trims normal when warm.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Walnut blast carbon cleaning procedure performed on cylinders 1 and 2 intake ports per Ford TSB 21-2441. Post-blast borescope: intake valves clear. Cold start cycle x4 — no misfire. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 25 miles. P0316 and cylinder-specific misfires not present. Customer advised on carbon cleaning interval every 40,000 miles.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0316 on 5.0 Coyote — cold start carbon misfire is a known GDI issue. Reference TSB 21-2441. Walnut blast is the correct fix — chemical cleaners are not effective for valve carbon deposits.
common_mistakes: Using chemical cleaner instead of walnut blast for valve carbon
Not performing multiple cold start cycles post-repair
Failing to document TSB reference number in C3

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Check engine light on for 3 weeks. No power loss. Customer had emissions test coming up.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0420 — catalyst efficiency bank 1. Post-cat O2 switching frequency: 0.41Hz (spec <0.1Hz for healthy catalyst). Pre-cat lambda: normal at 0.98-1.02. Upstream exhaust system inspection: no leaks. Mileage 76,200 — outside powertrain warranty but within federal 8-year/80,000-mile emissions warranty. VIN confirms coverage.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced catalytic converter bank 1 (Part #04E131690AA) under federal emissions warranty. ODIS drive cycle programmed and completed — 3 cold starts, 45 minutes mixed urban/highway. P0420 monitor status: complete — passed. Post-cat O2 waveform: flat 0.68V. Vehicle passed emissions test post-repair.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0420 on aging VAG — always check VIN against federal 8/80 emissions warranty before quoting customer. Many P0420 repairs on vehicles 6-8 years old are covered and technicians miss this.
common_mistakes: Not checking federal emissions warranty coverage by VIN
Insufficient ODIS drive cycle — monitor not completing
Missing post-repair O2 waveform documentation

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Rough idle when engine at operating temperature. CEL on. Customer states idle improved when he disconnected the EGR valve.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs P0401 — EGR flow insufficient and P0403 — EGR circuit open/range. EGR valve position sensor signal erratic — 0.2V to 4.8V noise (spec: smooth 0.5V-4.5V sweep). EGR valve resistance: open circuit between pins 2 and 4 (spec 20-40 ohms). Internal electrical failure confirmed. EGR passage carbon deposit minimal — passage clear per borescope.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced EGR valve assembly (Part #25620-31020). Cleared DTCs. EGR position signal: smooth 0.52V closed to 4.35V fully open — within spec. Idle quality improved — 710 RPM steady. Road tested 14 miles. P0401 and P0403 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0401 + P0403 together on Toyota — always measure EGR valve internal resistance before condemning the passage or cleaning. Electrical failure and carbon blockage require different repairs.
common_mistakes: Cleaning EGR passage without testing valve electrically
Not recording EGR position signal waveform quality
Missing internal resistance measurement in C2

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Engine idle hunting between 500 RPM and 1,200 RPM since new. Dealer addressed it once previously — issue returned.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0507 — IAC system high RPM. Throttle body bore carbon: confirmed present, 2.1mm deposit on plate edge. Idle air control adaptation value: maxed out at 100% (spec <80%). Previous workshop notes indicate throttle cleaning performed 6 months ago — reaccumulation noted. MBUX service record reviewed. Potential underlying oil blow-by issue causing rapid recontamination.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Throttle body cleaned per MB workshop instructions. PCV valve and oil separator inspected — oil separator membrane ruptured, excess crankcase vapors routing through intake. Replaced oil separator (Part #A2820100130). Throttle body recleaned post-oil-separator repair. IAC relearn via XENTRY. Idle 720 RPM stable. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 20 miles — no hunting. Customer advised of likely 12-month re-check.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Recurring P0507 after throttle clean — always investigate root cause of carbon reaccumulation. On M282, ruptured PCV oil separator membrane causes rapid throttle recontamination within 6,000 miles.
common_mistakes: Cleaning throttle body without checking PCV system
Not reviewing previous repair history
Closing job without identifying root cause of recontamination

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Start/stop not working. 'Battery charge too low for Start/Stop' message. Mild hybrid assist feels absent. 12V battery warning light on.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs P0562 system voltage low and P0A7F 48V mild hybrid battery SOC low. 12V battery: 490 CCA tested vs 680 CCA rated — failed. 48V MHEV battery: measured 38.2V (spec >43V minimum for Start/Stop enable). Alternator output: 14.1V — within spec. 48V belt starter-generator output normal at 47.9V. Root cause: degraded 12V battery causing auxiliary power drain that depressed 48V net.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced 12V AGM battery (Part #61219399367, 105Ah). Registered via ISTA. 48V system fully recovered to 46.8V within 15 minutes of engine run. Cleared DTCs. Start/stop functional. MHEV assist confirmed via 48V live data — regen braking 8.2kW measured. Road tested 25 miles.
repair_cases.technician_notes: 48V MHEV systems: a failing 12V battery can suppress the 48V net and appear as hybrid system fault. Always check 12V battery health first on mild hybrid voltage complaints. ISTA battery registration is mandatory.
common_mistakes: Investigating 48V system without testing 12V battery first
Not registering new battery via ISTA
Missing 48V recovery voltage documentation post-repair

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Car intermittently stalls at low speed. Hard restart after stall. No warning prior to stall. TPMS and ESP lights illuminate after stall event.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs P0340 (intake cam sensor circuit bank 1) and P0365 (exhaust cam sensor circuit bank 1). Both sensors signal loss during stall event freeze frame. Wiring harness inspection: both cam sensor leads pass through same conduit on cam cover — conduit chafed through, both signal wires have intermittent short to ground. Resistance both signal lines to ground: 4.2 ohms (spec: infinite).
repair_cases.c3_correction: Repaired both cam sensor signal wires with heat-shrink solder connectors. Relocated harness routing, added 200mm split loom protection over conduit exit point. Both signal lines retested: infinite resistance to ground. Cleared DTCs. Road test 20 miles including low-speed maneuvers — no stall, no DTCs. Secondary lights cleared.

parts_used.description: Wiring repair kit VAG 0.5mm heat shrink connectors

repair_cases.technician_notes: Dual cam sensor codes at same time on VAG — look for shared wiring issue before replacing both sensors. Common failure point: shared conduit on EA888 cam cover.
common_mistakes: Ordering both cam sensors without tracing wiring
Not testing signal line insulation resistance to ground
Forgetting to add loom protection over repair point

repair_cases.c1_complaint: MIL illuminated. Customer detected no fuel smell. No drivability issues. Car is regularly stored in heated garage.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0442 — small EVAP leak. EVAP smoke test: smoke visible at charcoal canister vent valve O-ring — pinhole crack visible under magnification. Canister body structurally intact. Fuel cap: passes test at 1.2 PSI. EVAP system pressure decay test: holds 0.8 PSI for 60 seconds (spec >1.0 PSI for 90 seconds — marginal).
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced charcoal canister vent valve O-ring kit (Part #99660221102). Smoke test repeated — no leak visible. Pressure decay: 1.1 PSI held for 120 seconds — passes. Cleared DTCs. Drive cycle including EVAP monitor conditions — P0442 monitor complete and passed. No fault present.

parts_used.description: Charcoal canister vent valve O-ring kit

repair_cases.technician_notes: P0442 on Porsche — heated garage storage with temperature cycling accelerates O-ring degradation. Always smoke test from canister vent side as well as fuel cap side. Record pressure decay rate, not just pass/fail.
common_mistakes: Only smoke testing from fuel cap — missing canister vent side
Not recording pressure decay numbers, only pass/fail
Replacing canister assembly when only O-ring is needed

repair_cases.c1_complaint: MIL on. Customer states vehicle uses slightly more fuel than usual. No other complaints.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0430 — catalyst efficiency below threshold bank 2. Post-cat O2 bank 2: switching 0.5Hz (spec: flat ±0.05V on healthy catalyst). Mileage 84,200 — outside 8/80 federal emissions warranty by 4,200 miles. Pre-cat bank 2 O2 function normal. No upstream exhaust leaks. Catalyst internally fragmented — confirmed by rattling sound under vehicle on cold start.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced catalytic converter bank 2 (Part #25051-31021). Customer pay — outside federal warranty. Post-replacement post-cat O2 bank 2: flat at 0.67V. Drive cycle completed. P0430 monitor passed. Fuel economy improvement expected over 1,000-mile break-in. Noise on cold start eliminated.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0430 on 1GR-FE at high mileage — physically inspect for internal catalyst fragmentation (rattle). If outside 8/80 window, customer pays. Document mileage vs warranty threshold clearly in C2.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — 4,200 miles outside federal 8-year/80,000-mile emissions warranty.
common_mistakes: Not physically checking for catalyst rattle on high-mileage engines
Not documenting exact mileage vs warranty threshold in C2
Missing post-replacement drive cycle verification

repair_cases.c1_complaint: CEL on. Diesel particulate filter warning. Power is reduced above 60 MPH. Black smoke from exhaust on hard acceleration.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs P0401 EGR flow insufficient and P040F EGR B cooler performance. EGR cooler efficiency: coolant delta-T 18C across cooler (spec >35C delta). EGR valve position: responding correctly 0-95%. EGR cooler flow restriction confirmed — internal soot and oil deposit blockage. Exhaust back pressure 0.35 bar at 2000 RPM (spec <0.15 bar).
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced EGR cooler (Part #04L131512N) and EGR valve gaskets (Part #04L131521D). Post-replacement cooler efficiency: delta-T 42C — within spec. Exhaust back pressure: 0.08 bar. DPF forced regeneration performed via ODIS. Cleared all DTCs. Road tested 30 miles motorway — no smoke, full power restored. P0401 and P040F not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0401 on TDI diesel — measure EGR cooler delta-T before condemning valve. Blocked cooler restricts flow and causes identical DTC to stuck valve. DPF forced regen is mandatory after EGR cooler replacement to clear accumulated soot.
common_mistakes: Replacing EGR valve without testing cooler delta-T
Skipping DPF forced regeneration after cooler replacement
Not recording exhaust back pressure values

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Multiple warning lights on after cold start: M traction control, DSC, steering assist, heads-up display offline. All clear after engine restart in 60% of occurrences.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC U0100 — lost communication with ECM/PCM, intermittent. HS-CAN bus oscilloscope capture during fault: dominant voltage dipping to 1.1V (spec 1.5V). CAN bus termination resistance: 120 ohms each end — correct. Traced HS-CAN circuit — intermittent open in shielded CAN cable at harness routing clip under battery tray. Cable shield broken, causing noise-induced dominant voltage dropout.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced 350mm section of HS-CAN shielded cable at battery tray routing (Part #61126987788). CAN shielding integrity confirmed with insulation resistance tester — infinite resistance shield to signal wire. Dominant voltage: stable 1.5V on oscilloscope. Cleared DTCs. Cold start x5 — no warning lights. Road tested 30 miles. No U0100 recurrence.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Intermittent U0100 on BMW — requires oscilloscope to capture transient CAN bus dropout. Standard multimeter insufficient. Shield integrity check with insulation resistance tester is the key diagnostic step.
common_mistakes: Diagnosing intermittent U0100 without oscilloscope
Not checking CAN cable shield integrity
Clearing DTCs without capturing waveform evidence for warranty

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Airbag warning light on for 2 days. All other systems normal. No recent accident or impact reported.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC B1000 — control unit N2/7 (restraint systems control unit) internal fault. XENTRY diagnostic: RCM (restraint control module) internal voltage regulator fault code, non-resettable. Communication with RCM stable — no network fault. Module log shows fault first occurred during 48V system voltage transient event. RCM self-test fails on internal power circuit.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced restraint control module (Part #A2239007601) per MB SIB-68.80-P-051717. MB engineering authorization number obtained (required for SRS component replacement). Module coded and adapted via XENTRY. SRS self-test: all pass. Airbag warning light extinguished. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 10 miles. No fault return.
repair_cases.technician_notes: B1000 on MB SRS — obtain MB engineering authorization before condemning RCM. Module must be coded via XENTRY post-replacement. Document authorization number in C3 — mandatory for warranty claim.
common_mistakes: Not obtaining MB engineering authorization before SRS module replacement
Skipping module coding via XENTRY
Forgetting to record authorization number in C3

repair_cases.c1_complaint: ABS light and stability control light on. Customer states ABS activated at low speed on dry road — unusual behavior.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs C0035 and C0040 — left front wheel speed sensor circuit, and right front wheel speed sensor circuit respectively. Live data: LF wheel speed sensor output: 0 MPH while moving (signal dropout). RF sensor: 47 MPH normal. ABS module triggering false ABS cycle on LF due to zero-speed signal. LF sensor harness inspected: connector corrosion — pins 1 and 3 green corrosion, >50 ohms resistance (spec <1 ohm). RF sensor circuit inspected — similar corrosion, 28 ohms resistance.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced both front wheel speed sensor connectors and cleaned sensor mounting faces (Part #F2GZ-2C519-A x2). Post-repair: LF sensor resistance 0.3 ohms, RF 0.4 ohms. Live data: both sensors tracking correctly at all speeds. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 10 miles including low-speed ABS trigger maneuver — no false activation. C0035 and C0040 not present.
repair_cases.technician_notes: C0035 and C0040 together on Ford — connector corrosion is more common than sensor failure on 2019-2022 Edge. Check circuit resistance before ordering sensors. Document resistance values for both sensors in C2.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — corrosion damage from aftermarket carwash with high-pressure underbody wash.
common_mistakes: Replacing wheel speed sensors without measuring circuit resistance
Not inspecting both sides when one sensor is faulty
Failing to document resistance values in C2

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Transmission shudder felt between 35-45 MPH under light throttle. Only noticed at highway speeds. No warning light initially — now D4 flashes.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTC P0700 and P0741 — torque converter clutch performance/stuck off. Torque converter lockup slip at 40 MPH cruise: 180 RPM slip (spec <30 RPM slip). ATF condition: milky appearance, emulsified — water contamination confirmed. ATF dipstick shows fluid level 40mm overfull. Customer's previous independent shop overfilled and used incorrect Dexron ATF (Honda Z1 fluid required).
repair_cases.c3_correction: ATF drain — contaminated fluid removed (6.2L drained). Torque converter drained separately (additional 2.8L). Refilled with Honda DW-1 ATF x4 flushes (Part #08200-9008) until fluid ran clean amber. Torque converter lockup slip rechecked: 12 RPM — within spec. Cleared DTCs. Road tested 25 miles at highway speed — no shudder. D4 not flashing.
repair_cases.technician_notes: P0741 shudder on Honda — always check ATF condition and verify correct fluid type before condemning torque converter. Wrong fluid or water contamination causes identical symptoms to mechanical TCC failure.
repair_cases.warranty_note: Customer pay — incorrect fluid installed by independent shop. Not covered under Honda warranty.
common_mistakes: Condemning torque converter without checking ATF fluid type and condition
Not measuring torque converter lockup slip RPM
Missing fluid overfill as a diagnostic clue

repair_cases.c1_complaint: Multiple warnings on MMI: 'Charging system fault', 'Driver assistance systems unavailable', 'Electric drive system limited'. Vehicle driveable but in limp mode at 30 MPH max.
repair_cases.c2_cause: DTCs U0100 lost communication with ECM and B1000 restraint control fault. ODIS scan: high-voltage battery management module (BMS) not communicating on CAN bus. HV system isolation test: 4.2 MOhm (spec >500 kOhm — passes). 12V auxiliary system voltage: 10.8V (spec 12.4V) — 12V battery critically low, causing CAN bus brownout affecting both drivetrain and SRS modules. CCA test: 12V battery 260 CCA vs 520 CCA rated — failed.
repair_cases.c3_correction: Replaced 12V auxiliary battery (Part #4KE915105B, 70Ah AGM). Registered via ODIS. HV system re-enabled per Audi HV safety protocol — HV interlock loop verified closed, pre-charge circuit tested. All CAN bus modules online within 90 seconds of 12V recovery. BMS communication restored. Cleared all DTCs. Limp mode cleared. Road tested 20 miles — full power, all driver assistance systems operational.
repair_cases.technician_notes: Multiple CAN bus faults plus BMS communication loss on Audi BEV — always check 12V auxiliary battery first. EV platforms are extremely sensitive to 12V undervoltage. HV safety protocol must be followed even when servicing only the 12V system. ODIS battery registration mandatory.
common_mistakes: Investigating HV system without testing 12V auxiliary battery first
Skipping HV interlock loop verification after 12V work
Not registering new 12V battery via ODIS on BEV platform
